summary: We recommend vendoring the reference MCP server as a git submodule at the path mcp-server because that keeps the spec and the implementation colocated for contributors while preserving a single source of truth at opensubagents outcomes-mcp. The tradeoff against a vendored copy is that submodules add one extra clone step but they explicitly point at a commit SHA which makes the pin auditable and forces readers to go to the canonical source for issues. The git modules file now records the submodule pointer and docs mcp-server md documents the trust boundary and the bump procedure. This closes queue item B3 on the outcomes-mcp orchestrator queue.

claims.statement: git submodule pointer at path mcp-server resolves to opensubagents outcomes-mcp at a pinned SHA because the git modules file lists path equal to mcp-server and url equal to the canonical repo URL and the index points at a specific commit object.

citations.title: gitmodules file declares the mcp-server submodule
outcomes-mcp main branch the submodule is pinned to

claims.caveats: The SHA the submodule is pinned at advances only when a contributor runs git submodule update remote and opens a bump PR; this is intentional friction so the spec repo only takes pin bumps explicitly.

claims.statement: docs mcp-server md explains the trust boundary and the update procedure with the submodule update remote command because the file documents that submodule contents do not run during clone or checkout and that bumps go through a normal PR on this repo.

citations.title: docs mcp-server md trust boundary and update procedure
Git book submodules reference behind the trust statements

claims.caveats: If a future contributor wants to vendor the source directly instead of submoduling that would be a follow-up ADR not a B3 revival.

claims.statement: the submodule contents are not evaluated by this repo outcome-gate only the pointer SHA is committed because the gate runs against changed paths under outcomes star outcome json and star report json and not against arbitrary subtrees.

citations.title: outcome-gate workflow paths filter only outcomes star outcome json and star report json
docs mcp-server md states the trust boundary explicitly

claims.caveats: Adding the submodule changes the gitmodules file and adds a pointer entry; that is the only audit surface this PR introduces.

open_questions: Should outcomes also vendor a sdk-python copy or sdk-typescript copy as submodules from the spec repo into outcomes-mcp for symmetry or is the one-way pointer from outcomes to outcomes-mcp sufficient?
methodology_notes: Submodule added via git submodule add then docs mcp-server md authored with a one-paragraph trust statement and a code block for the bump procedure. Bootstrap pair verified locally against the python HeuristicVerifier before pushing.
